task_completion.description: The BraTS MRI volume is loaded and visible in the slice views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red, Yellow, or Green slice views show a brain MRI scan (grayscale brain structures) rather than being empty or black.

task_completion.description: A Line markup (ruler) has been created in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored line with two endpoints is visible on the 2D slice view. It may be labeled 'L' or 'Line'.

task_completion.description: One endpoint of the line touches the tumor boundary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One end of the measurement line is placed on the edge of the bright, hyperintense (white) region representing the tumor.

task_completion.description: The other endpoint of the line touches the brain's midline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The second endpoint is placed on the interhemispheric fissure (the dark central vertical gap separating the left and right brain hemispheres).

task_completion.description: The measurement line is oriented correctly to measure the shortest distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line connects the tumor to the midline in a roughly horizontal (left-right) direction, perpendicular to the midline.

task_completion.description: The distance measurement value is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric value (in mm) is visible next to the line markup or in the Markups module table.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the screenshot as requested.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the 'Screen Capture' module, a 'Save' dialog, or the file explorer shows 'midline_distance.png' being saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Slicer GUI tools for measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is seen interacting with the Markups toolbar or slice view, rather than the measurement appearing solely via Python console commands.

integrity.description: The task is performed in the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard 3D Slicer interface (module panel on left, slice views on right/center).

integrity.description: The measurement is a real Slicer markup object.
integrity.visual_evidence: The line has control points (small dots at ends) and a label, distinguishing it from a drawn-on annotation or external image edit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The tumor appears as a bright (white/hyperintense) region in the brain scan. The midline is the dark vertical gap (interhemispheric fissure) running down the center of the brain. The measurement line should connect the edge of the bright tumor to this central dark gap. The line should be approximately horizontal (left-right orientation). The distance value is dynamic but should be plausible for the gap shown (typically 10-50mm).
